subject: URGENT: Emergency Bursary Request & Financial Hardship - Joel Palk-Ricard (026004)
body:
Dear Financial Aid Office,

I am writing to formally request an emergency bursary and to discuss a critical financial situation that is currently blocking my academic progress for the upcoming Winter 2026 term.

Currently, my student loans for the Fall 2025 term have been significantly delayed, leaving me with a balance owing to the university and several months behind on essential living expenses. This outstanding balance has created a severe procedural block: because of the hold on my account, I am unable to modify my registration or withdraw from courses if necessary, which previously led to academic penalties in 2023.

I am requesting any available emergency funding to cover immediate costs while the loan processing is finalized. Additionally, I am requesting a temporary waiver of the registration hold so that I may enroll in the remote courses required for my degree convergence (MATH 1311, HLTH 1011), ensuring my graduation timeline remains intact for Spring 2027.

Thank you for your immediate attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
Joel Palk-Ricard (026004)

subject: Appeal for Retroactive Withdrawal and Correction of Scholastic Standing - Joel Palk-Ricard (026004)
body:
Dear Registrar’s Office,

I am writing to formally appeal several "F" grades from the 2023 academic year and to request a correction to my provincial scholastic standing.

**The 2023 Failures: A Procedural Block**
During the 2023 academic year, I attempted to withdraw from my courses due to extenuating circumstances. However, I was prevented from doing so by the university system because I had an outstanding balance at the time. This resulted in a "Catch-22" where I was academically forced to fail courses I was no longer attending and was physically barred from dropping. I am requesting that these grades be converted to "W" (Withdrawn) marks, as the failure was a result of a systemic barrier rather than academic performance.

**Scholastic Standing Correction**
I also believe there is an error regarding my provincial scholastic standing. Given my recent academic performance in Fall 2025 (completion of COMM 3611, VMCS 1201, and INDG courses), I request a review of my standing to ensure it accurately reflects my progress toward graduation.

Correcting these errors is essential for my continued eligibility for student financial assistance and my path to completing my BSc in Biology in 2027.

Best regards,
Joel Palk-Ricard (026004)

subject: Loan Account Review Request – Academic Disruptions and Pending University Appeal
body:
Dear National Student Loans Service Centre,

I am writing to request a review of my Canada Student Loan account in light of documented academic disruptions at my institution, Mount Allison University, and a formal academic appeal that is currently underway there.

In 2023, institutional holds and systemic barriers prevented me from properly withdrawing from courses, leading to an unfair reflection of my academic standing which subsequently affected my loan eligibility. I am currently in the process of a formal appeal with the university to have these records corrected to reflect my good-faith academic efforts and intended withdrawal.

I request that my account be flagged for review to prevent any negative repercussions while this institutional correction is processed, and to ensure my continued eligibility for the Spring 2026/2027 terms as I work toward my degree completion in Spring 2027.

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
Joel Palk-Ricard (026004)
